response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned polyglot engineer with strong craftsmanship practices and broad full-stack experience, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/JavaScript, and while Java appears in his skills list, it surfaces only in minor contexts (robotic arms, mobile orchestration tooling) with no evidence of deep JVM knowledge, Spring ecosystem work, or Kotlin experience. The closest IDE-adjacent experience is a brief stint writing C/C++ components for a C IDE at National Instruments, which is architecturally distant from enterprise JVM tooling. None of the required technical focus areas — Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, build tools, application server integration, or IDE internals — are substantiated in his history.
strengths: Demonstrated commitment to software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development practiced consistently across roles
Prior experience writing tooling and developer infrastructure (NI LabWindows/CVI C IDE, MCP code generators, Fluent-Asserts, Trial test runner) shows genuine interest in developer-experience work
Exposure to large-scale, high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby) indicates ability to reason about performance-critical codebases
concerns: No demonstrated Java or Kotlin depth; Java is listed as a skill but plays no substantive role in any described project or responsibility
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience across 15+ years of history
IDE internals experience is limited to a short C/C++ role on a C IDE; no JVM tooling, IntelliJ platform, or language-server work is present

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling interest and craftsmanship practices
